metaTitle: Table Themes — Style Your Table in One Click | Tablesmit
metaDescription: Six table themes — Default, Minimal, Dark Header, Striped, Academic, Monochrome — apply a complete visual style instantly. No manual formatting needed.
heroHeadline: A complete visual style in one click.
heroSubtext: Six carefully designed themes apply header colours, border styles, row treatments, and typography in one selection. Your table looks considered without the effort.

benefits.heading: Six themes built for different contexts
benefits.body: Default for general use. Minimal for clean embedding. Dark Header for bold reports. Striped for long data tables. Academic for research and publishing. Monochrome for print-first output.

benefits.heading: One click — complete treatment
benefits.body: A theme sets the header background, header text colour, row alternation, and border weight simultaneously. No per-cell formatting. No manual colour picking.

benefits.heading: Themes export faithfully
benefits.body: Every theme exports correctly to PDF, Excel, and PNG. The Academic grey header, the Striped alternating rows, the Dark Header navy — all preserved in every format.

benefits.heading: Override after applying
benefits.body: A theme is a starting point, not a constraint. After applying, you can change the header colour, adjust borders, or switch content colour without leaving the theme system.

themes.name: Default
themes.description: Primary blue header, white rows, standard border. The right choice for most reports and documents.

themes.name: Minimal
themes.description: No header colour, hairline borders, clean white throughout. Designed to sit inside a document without drawing attention to itself.
themes.bestFor: Blog posts, embedding in documents, editorial content

themes.name: Dark Header
themes.description: Deep navy header with white text, light grey alternating rows. Strong visual hierarchy for comparison and reference tables.

themes.name: Striped
themes.description: Primary blue header with alternating white and light grey rows. Improves readability across wide tables with many rows.
themes.bestFor: Data tables, financial reports, spreadsheet-style output

themes.name: Academic
themes.description: Light grey header with standard borders. Matches the visual conventions of academic journals and research publications.
themes.bestFor: Research papers, literature reviews, journal submissions

themes.name: Monochrome
themes.description: Greyscale throughout — no colour accents. Designed for tables that will be printed or appear in black and white.
themes.bestFor: Print documents, legal filings, formal reports

steps.heading: Open the Theme menu
steps.body: Click the Theme button in the toolbar to open the theme picker.

steps.heading: Select a theme
steps.body: Click any theme to apply it instantly. The table updates in real time — you can preview each theme before deciding.

steps.heading: Fine-tune if needed
steps.body: Use the Header colour picker in the sidebar to adjust the header background. Themes are starting points — every element remains customisable.

useCases: Applying Academic theme to a literature review table for a journal submission
Using Minimal theme for a table embedded in a blog post or newsletter
Choosing Striped theme for a long financial data table to improve row readability
Using Monochrome theme for tables that will appear in a printed or black-and-white document

_codex_notes.note: The 'themes' array is additional data specific to this page. In FeatureDetailPage, if page.themes exists, render a ThemePreviewGrid component between the benefits and steps sections. Each theme card shows: name, description, bestFor, and a small SVG thumbnail preview of the theme's visual style (header colour, row treatment).
